notes: Diverse baseline test set covering the four main workflows (build, review, polish, redesign) plus router-selection cases. Run a baseline (no skill) and a with-skill subagent per eval and compare. See SKILL.md 'Standard Workflows' for expected file loads per prompt.

evals.name: build-form-component
evals.prompt: Build a sign-up form in React + Tailwind with email, password, and 'I agree to terms' checkbox. Production-quality: state completeness, accessibility, mobile-first, no AI-tell visual patterns.
evals.expected_output: A single .tsx file with labeled inputs, autocomplete attrs, inputmode, disabled state, error state, focus-visible rings, touch-target sizing, and no hardcoded palette classes.

evals.name: review-dashboard-pr
evals.prompt: Review this dashboard component for UX issues. Cite file:line for every finding. Use Critical / Important / Opportunity buckets.
evals.expected_output: Structured review using the exact Output Format template. State-completeness grep should be run before claiming completeness. Severity should match the user-blocking-impact principle (no over-claiming Critical for missing focus-visible when there is no outline suppression).

evals.name: polish-modal-motion
evals.prompt: This dialog feels janky. Polish the open/close motion and the focus handoff. The project ships shipping-style polish, not playful CSS-art.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Jakub Krehel (production polish), not Jhey (experimental). Specifies easing curves and durations as tokens, includes prefers-reduced-motion, uses transform/opacity (never layout properties).

evals.name: redesign-landing-hero
evals.prompt: Our landing-page hero looks like a 2018 SaaS template. Make it feel current without rebuilding from scratch. Diagnose first, then fix.
evals.expected_output: Follows Scan → Diagnose → Fix. Diagnose phase cites concrete AI-tells from 06. Fix proposes Bento 2.0 or anti-slop creative direction from 09. Does not rebuild from scratch.

evals.name: router-discipline-pure-data-table
evals.prompt: Review this read-only data table that fetches rows and renders them. No animation, no forms, no modals.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT load 07 (no motion), 05 (no landing-page), 09 (no aesthetic ask), 07a/07b/07c. Demonstrates lazy-load discipline — small token footprint.

evals.name: router-discipline-motion-only
evals.prompt: Audit the page-transition animation only. Tell me which timing / easing / approach is off.
evals.expected_output: Loads 07 (motion framework), then routes to one designer reference (07a / 07b / 07c) based on project polish level. Does NOT load 10 (this is not a code review), 03, 04, or 06.
